title: IT Professional Elective — Finals Reviewer
subtitle: Modules 1, 2 & 4 + Midterm coverage. Select a version below to begin.

questions.t: A beginner wants to make a webpage interactive by adding clickable buttons and animations without using a complex programming language. Which technology is MOST appropriate?
A programmer writes JavaScript code but receives errors because the structure of the statements is incorrect. Which concept should the programmer review?
A developer needs to store a student's score so it can be updated later during program execution. Which JavaScript concept should be used?
In JavaScript, the value "Hello World" is directly written in the code and never changes. What is this value called?
A developer writes one block of code that can calculate totals whenever it is called multiple times in a program. What JavaScript concept is being demonstrated?
A function named computeGrade(score) accepts a value inside the parentheses when called. What is score called?
A programmer creates a function without giving it a name because it will only be used once. What type of function is this?
A programmer stores a function inside a variable so it can be passed around and reused later. Which concept does this demonstrate?
A function is successfully called before its declaration appears in the code. Which JavaScript behavior allows this to happen?
A function runs automatically immediately after it is declared without being called manually. Which type of function is this?
A developer wants to copy all elements of one array into another array using ...arrayName. What JavaScript feature is being used?
A programmer uses statements to determine whether code should repeat, stop, or branch into another path. What category do these statements belong to?
A grading system checks whether a student passed, failed, or received honors based on conditions. Which category of control statement is MOST appropriate?
A developer needs to display the numbers 1 to 100 repeatedly in sequence. Which control structure should be used?
A menu program must display options at least once before checking whether the user wants to continue. Which loop is MOST suitable?
A developer places one if statement inside another if statement to validate multiple conditions. What is this structure called?
A programmer wants to simplify multiple if-else conditions that check the value of one variable with many possible outcomes. Which statement is the BEST choice?
A student says HTML is a programming language because it creates websites. Which statement BEST corrects the misunderstanding?
choices: HTML is used to create databases.
HTML is only used for styling websites.
HTML is a markup language used to structure web pages.
questions.a: HTML is a markup language used to structure web pages.

questions.t: A web developer wants users to move from one webpage to another by clicking text or images. Which HTML concept makes this possible?

A programmer uses tags like `

` and `

` to organize webpage content. What HTML concept is being demonstrated?
A beginner combines HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in one project. Which BEST describes the role of HTML in web development?
choices: HTML connects the website to the server.
HTML provides the structure or skeleton of the webpage.
questions.a: HTML provides the structure or skeleton of the webpage.

questions.t: What is the main purpose of JavaScript in web development?
choices: To add interactivity to web pages
questions.a: To add interactivity to web pages

questions.t: JavaScript is considered a __________ language.
Which HTML tag is used to insert JavaScript code?
JavaScript is an __________ language.
Which of the following is a valid JavaScript identifier?
Functions in JavaScript are used to __________.
Which keyword is used to define a function?
What will addNumbers(10,6) return if the function adds two numbers?
What is an arrow function?
choices: A shorter syntax for writing functions
questions.a: A shorter syntax for writing functions

questions.t: Which symbol is used in arrow functions?
Arrow functions are __________.
Parameters are __________.
What will happen if no arguments are passed to a function with default values?
Which operator is used to spread array elements?
What does the return keyword do?
choices: Sends a value back to the caller
questions.a: Sends a value back to the caller

questions.t: What is an IIFE?
Which statement is used to test conditions?
Which statement executes code only when a condition is true?
The else statement is used to __________.
choices: Execute code when the condition is false
questions.a: Execute code when the condition is false

questions.t: A nested if statement means __________.
choices: an if statement inside another if statement
questions.a: an if statement inside another if statement

questions.t: Which statement is best for checking many possible values?
Which loop is commonly used when the number of iterations is known?
Which loop checks the condition before execution?
What tag is used to create an HTML form that collects user input?
Which HTML element creates a drop-down selection list?
A developer needs to mask the characters a user types into a login field. Which input type should be used?
A form uses `method="get"`. Where does the submitted data appear?
Which HTTP method sends form data in the request body, making it less visible than data sent in the URL?
A developer wants to show a quick debugging value in the browser's developer tools. Which method should be used?
Which JavaScript method writes content directly into the HTML document's output stream?
Which keyword declares a variable whose value cannot be reassigned?
The expression `new Function("a", "b", "return a + b")` uses which technique?
What does the `break` statement do when used inside a loop?
What does DOM stand for?
How does the DOM represent a webpage?
choices: As a flat list of strings
As a hierarchical tree of nodes
As a sequence of HTTP requests
questions.a: As a hierarchical tree of nodes

questions.t: Which object is the top-level parent in the browser's object hierarchy?
The `document` object is a child of which object?
Which DOM method returns the single element matching a given `id`?
What does `document.getElementsByTagName("p")` return?
choices:
An HTMLCollection of all `

` elements on the page
questions.a:
An HTMLCollection of all `

` elements on the page

questions.t: Which DOM method returns a NodeList of elements sharing the same `name` attribute, commonly used for form controls?
What is a major risk of calling `document.write()` after the page has finished loading?
choices: It writes to the console instead
It can overwrite the entire page
It has no effect at all
questions.a: It can overwrite the entire page

questions.t: Clicking a button, hovering over an element, and pressing a key are all examples of DOM:
Which of the following is something the DOM is NOT?
choices: A way to access and manipulate documents
A binary description of a document
questions.a: A binary description of a document

questions.t: What type of collection does `getElementsByClassName()` return?
Which DOM event is triggered by a keyboard interaction?
The `document.writeln()` method differs from `document.write()` by:
choices: Adding a newline after the output
questions.a: Adding a newline after the output

questions.t: Which DOM object represents `<a>` elements that contain an `href` attribute?
Which DOM object represents `<link>` elements in the HTML head?
The DOM provides methods for accessing and manipulating page elements through which object?
In the DOM, nodes may have _____ that respond to user actions like clicks and key presses.
An online shopping cart shows a number badge. When the user clicks "+", JavaScript updates the count without reloading the page. Which DOM method is most likely used?
Which DOM event fires when a form is submitted?
Which DOM event fires when the user moves focus away from an input field?
A beginner wants to make a webpage interactive by adding clickable buttons and animations without using a complex programming language. The technology is _____.
A programmer receives errors because the structure of JavaScript statements is incorrect. This concept is called _____.
In JavaScript, _____ are used to store data values that can be updated during program execution.
Fixed values like `42` or `"Hello World"` written directly in JavaScript code are called _____.
A reusable block of code designed to perform a specific task is called a _____.
The placeholders listed in a function's definition (inside the parentheses) are called _____.
A function with no name, typically assigned to a variable or passed as an argument, is called an _____ function.
An _____ function is a modern JavaScript feature that uses a shorter syntax (with `=>`) to write functions.
A _____ is a function stored inside a variable.
_____ is JavaScript's behavior of moving function declarations to the top of their scope before code execution.
An _____ is a function that runs automatically immediately after it is defined.
The _____ operator (`...`) expands an array or iterable into individual values.
_____ statements (like if, switch, loops) determine execution flow or paths.
Control structures that branch execution path based on boolean tests are called _____ control statements.
A _____ control structure repeats execution of a block of code a certain number of times or until a condition is met.
A _____ loop guarantees its code block runs at least once because the condition is checked after execution.
An if statement placed inside another if statement is called a _____ if statement.
The _____ statement in JavaScript evaluates an expression against multiple cases.
HTML stands for _____ Markup Language.
_____ refers to the clickable text links or media that connect different web pages together.
HTML uses markup _____ to structure and identify content elements on a webpage.
The primary role of HTML in web development is to define the _____ or skeleton of a webpage.
JavaScript was originally designed to add _____ to static HTML pages.
JavaScript is a lightweight, interpreted _____ language.
If x = -2, the code block else if (x < 0) will execute, outputting _____ to the console.
JavaScript code is embedded in HTML using the _____ tag.
Because scripts run directly without separate compilation, JavaScript is an _____ language.
Variables and function names in JavaScript are called _____.
In programming, _____ are used to perform specific tasks and encapsulate reusable statements.
The _____ keyword is used to declare a function.
Calling a function that adds two numbers with arguments 10 and 6 returns _____.
An arrow function provides a shorter _____ for writing functions in JavaScript.
Arrow functions use the _____ symbol in their declaration.
Unlike function declarations, arrow functions are _____ hoisted.
The function call `x(2,3)` where x is `(x, y) => x * y` evaluates to _____.
Function parameters act as _____ inside the function's code block.
If no arguments are passed, a function with defined parameter values uses _____ parameter values.
The _____ operator (`...`) is used to expand array elements into individual items.
The _____ keyword inside a function stops execution and sends a value back to the caller.
An Immediately Invoked Function Expression is also known as a _____ function.
An `if` statement is a type of _____ statement used to branch execution based on condition tests.
If x = 10, the condition `x > 5` evaluates to true, so the statement prints _____ to the console.
The _____ statement is used to execute a block of code only if a specified condition evaluates to true.
The _____ statement is used to execute a block of code when the `if` condition evaluates to false.
Putting one `if` statement inside another `if` statement is called _____ them.
The _____ statement is the best choice for checking one variable against many exact matching values.
If x = 7, the check `x % 2 == 0` evaluates to false, so the number is evaluated as _____.
The _____ loop is commonly used when the number of iterations is known in advance.
The _____ loop is an entry-controlled loop that checks the condition before executing the block.
A loop starting at 1, incrementing by 1, and continuing while i <= 3, will print _____.
The _____ tag defines an HTML form used to collect user input.
The _____ element creates a drop-down selection list in an HTML form.
The input type _____ masks characters as they are typed, commonly used for password fields.
When a form uses `method="get"`, the submitted data is appended to the _____.
When using the _____ method, form data is placed in the request body rather than the URL.
The JavaScript method _____ displays output in the browser's developer tools console.
The _____ method writes content directly into the HTML document's output stream.
The _____ keyword declares a variable whose value cannot be reassigned.
Creating a function dynamically using `new Function(...)` is using the Function _____ technique.
In a loop, the _____ statement exits the loop entirely.
DOM stands for _____ Object Model.
The DOM represents a webpage as a hierarchical _____ of nodes.
The _____ object is the top-level parent in the browser's object hierarchy.
In the browser's object hierarchy, the `document` object is a child of the _____ object.
The DOM method _____ returns the single element matching a given id.
The DOM method `document.getElementsByTagName("p")` returns an _____ of all matching elements on the page.
The DOM method _____ returns a NodeList of elements sharing the same name attribute.
Calling `document.write()` after the page has finished loading can _____ the entire existing page content.
Clicking a button, hovering over an element, and pressing a key are all examples of DOM _____.
The DOM is not a _____ description of a document.
The DOM method `getElementsByClassName()` returns a live _____ of elements sharing the class name.
The event _____ is fired when the user presses a key on the keyboard.
The method `document.writeln()` differs from `document.write()` by adding a _____ after the output.
The DOM _____ object represents `<a>` elements containing an `href` attribute.
The DOM _____ object represents `<link>` elements in the document header.
The DOM provides methods for accessing and manipulating page elements through the _____ object.
In the DOM, nodes may have event _____ that respond to user interactions.
To update a specific element on a webpage without reloading the page, developers commonly use the _____ method.
The DOM event _____ fires when the user submits an HTML form.
The DOM event _____ fires when an element loses focus.
